Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Experimental procedures explicitly list AtLEA3 as AT1G02820 and BnLEA3.1-3.4 as GenBank KP315905-KP315908.

Source_Species.reason: The paper studies homologs from Arabidopsis and Brassica napus.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana and Brassica napus are explicitly identifiable from the paper.

Applied_Species.reason: Transgenic experiments were performed in Arabidopsis and B. napus (oilseed rape).

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Applied species are Arabidopsis thaliana and Brassica napus.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Experimental procedures state Columbia ecotype for Arabidopsis and B. napus cv. Westar.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Columbia ecotype and Westar are the applied experimental backgrounds; the paper does not clearly define these as the source varieties of the cloned LEA3 genes.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results repeatedly measure seed oil content as the output trait; Figure 1h/j references TAG synthetic pathway, supporting triacylglycerol as the oil form.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Seed oil/TAG is a lipid class, consistent with the paper's framing of lipid accumulation.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Summary states lipids are the main economic factor in oil crops, i.e. the major storage/economic product.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Oil content was measured in mature seeds; the study focuses on seed oil accumulation.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Title and text identify LEA3 as late embryogenesis abundant group 3.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show LEA3 OE increased drought tolerance, oil content, seed size and TSW, while mutant/RNAi reduced them.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is supported by Results: OE increased oil in Arabidopsis and B. napus, atlea3/BnLEA-RNAi reduced oil and drought tolerance, complementation rescued phenotypes, and transcriptome/physiology linked effects to photosynthesis with FA synthesis up and degradation/oxidation down.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Experimental procedures state the selected BnLEA3 copies were chosen because prior studies showed relatively stable and higher expression in seeds and leaves during drought.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: RNA-seq compared leaves and siliques/seeds under short- and long-term drought in LEA differential-expression lines.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: The Discussion states many LEA proteins localize to cytosol and plastids and can diffuse into the nucleus, but this is presented as general/background for LEA proteins, not a direct experimental localization result for the tested LEA3 proteins in this paper.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods except exact wording are present: overexpression, T-DNA mutant, B. napus RNAi, complementation, qRT-PCR, RNA-seq, oil/FA profiling, and drought physiological assays.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: LACS9, LOX2, LIPASE1, ACPs, LTPs and SnRK-related components were discussed as differentially expressed/pathway-associated genes, but the paper does not show physical protein-protein interactions.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Results explicitly describe 35S CaMV and glycinin promoters for whole-plant and seed-specific overexpression vectors.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Experimental procedures define short-term 15% PEG treatment and long-term water withholding drought treatment.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results/Discussion support that LEA3 acts indirectly on lipid accumulation via photosynthetic efficiency, membrane stability, and reduced FA degradation/oxidation; hybrid reinforcement with VOC-OE is also shown. The 'not a catalytic enzyme or TF' part is not explicitly stated but is consistent with LEA protein identity.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Figure 3 text states OE seeds were larger than controls without morphological malformation; no tradeoff is reported.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Summary and Discussion emphasize improving oil accumulation and drought tolerance, and hybrids/seed-specific promoter results support engineering value.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq is extensively reported; no accession is provided in the paper, so 'accession NA' is consistent.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Experimental procedures explicitly list AtVOC as AT1G07645 and BnVOC1-4 as KP315909-KP315912.

Source_Species.reason: The paper studies VOC homologs from Arabidopsis and Brassica napus.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Arabidopsis thaliana and Brassica napus are the species used.

Applied_Species.reason: Functional tests were performed in Arabidopsis and oilseed rape/B. napus.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Applied species are Arabidopsis thaliana and Brassica napus.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Experimental procedures specify Columbia ecotype and Westar.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: These are experimental recipient backgrounds; the paper does not clearly state them as the source varieties for the cloned VOC genes.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: VOC is directly linked to increased seed oil content, and TAG pathway genes are discussed; thus seed oil/triacylglycerol is supported.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper frames oil/lipid as the major economic product in oil crops.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Oil accumulation was measured in seeds.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Title identifies VOC as vicinal oxygen chelate, and Discussion states the main subfamily of VOCs is GLY I.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support increased seed oil, drought tolerance, seed size and TSW in VOC-OE; RNAi lines show opposite trends. ROS/MG effects and FA degradation suppression are also reported.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All four points are supported by the Results/Discussion: OE increased oil, RNAi reduced oil and drought tolerance, VOC-OE altered H2O2/MG/MDA/GSH/GSSG and increased GLY I/II activities, and transcriptome analyses linked VOC to FA degradation, glyoxylate/dicarboxylate metabolism, and photosynthetic/light reaction terms.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Experimental procedures state selected BnVOC copies had relatively stable and higher expression in seeds and leaves during drought in previous studies.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper analyzes VOC differential expression effects in leaves and siliques/seeds under short- and long-term drought.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: Discussion explicitly states VOC subcellular location was nucleus, cell membrane, and cytosol, citing Figure S14.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are described in the paper: overexpression, RNAi, E. coli prokaryotic expression for MG tolerance, qRT-PCR, RNA-seq, oil/FA profiling, enzyme assays and metabolite assays.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: GLY I/GLY II, SOD1, LOX2 and photosystem/lipid genes are functionally associated or differentially expressed, but direct physical interactions with VOC are not demonstrated.

Applied_Promoters.reason: 35S CaMV and glycinin promoter constructs were explicitly used for VOC overexpression.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Drought treatments included short-term PEG and long-term water withholding.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper supports that VOC is considered within the GLY I-related VOC family and functions in MG detoxification/glyoxylate metabolism, while the focal phenotype is drought-responsive lipid accumulation rather than a canonical lipid-biosynthetic enzyme role; hybrid reinforcement with LEA3 is also shown.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results note larger OE seeds without morphological malformation and do not report clear negative tradeoffs.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The study concludes VOC is useful for improving both oil accumulation and drought tolerance, and hybrid stacking with LEA is supported.

Omics_Data.reason: RNA-seq transcriptome analyses are central to the study, and no accession number is provided.
